raw_text: Spotify has completely lost sight of what made the app useful in the first place and somehow managed to turn itself into Pandora while pretending it's still Spotify. For many users, the entire appeal of Spotify was being able to choose what music you wanted to hear, when you wanted to hear it. Recently, they’ve made it so free users can't reliably select specific songs and can't even jump to a particular part of a song. At that point, it’s not a music library anymore.

For many users, the entire appeal of Spotify was being able to choose what music you wanted to hear, when you wanted to hear it. Recently, they’ve made it so free users can't reliably select specific songs and can't even jump to a particular part of a song. At that point, it’s not a music library anymore.

raw_text: Very good with premium, but terrible with the free version. Practically useless if you don't pay them every month. With premium, however, it is about the best music app available. The free version just contains a TON of advertisements and only allows you 6 skips an hour, whilst also recommending you songs, so if you get a random song you dislike, you can't skip it. The rating fluctuates depending on whether or not you have premium. I understand the 6 skips, but the recommended songs? Too far.
